title: lambertia rariflora subsp. lutea
intro: Lambertia rariflora subsp. lutea, commonly known as the yellow alpine pea, is a flowering plant species native to the southeastern regions of Australia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, with reported medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Lambertia rariflora subsp. lutea may include reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and potentially, antimicrobial protection. Herbal preparations often involve infusions or decoctions of the plant's leaves and flowers, which can be used to make teas, salves, or other topical treatments.
